title: Palantir Stock Is Taking a Hit: These Key Figures From the AI Company's Earnings Report Highlight Why I'm Still Not Buying
text: The article examines why Palantir Technologies stock declined nearly 7% following its earnings announcement, despite impressive revenue growth. While the company reported record 85% year-over-year revenue expansion in Q1, the analyst identifies concerning trends in forward-looking metrics. Q1 revenue reached $1.63 billion, marking the highest growth rate since going public. U.S. business segment grew 104% year-over-year with operating margins hitting an impressive 60%. However, closed total contract value growth dropped from 138% to 61% in a single quarter as a warning sign. Additionally, U.S. commercial remaining deal value growth has decelerated over three consecutive quarters from 199% to 145% to 112%. Even after the 20%+ decline in 2026, the stock trades at a price-to-sales ratio in the low 60s, leaving minimal room for operational missteps. The analyst suggests the valuation assumes years of continued exceptional performance that may not materialize. The author concludes by remaining skeptical, cautioning that momentum metrics could deteriorate faster than investors expect.
